creator: sobomax@FreeBSD.org
text:
"Dmitry S. Sivachenko" wrote:

> On Mon, Sep 03, 2001 at 02:20:12PM -0700, Maxim Sobolev wrote:
> > The following reply was made to PR ports/30292; it has been noted by GNATS.
> >
> > From: Maxim Sobolev <sobomax@FreeBSD.org>
> > To: will@FreeBSD.org, FreeBSD-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.org
> > Cc:
> > Subject: Re: ports/30292: QT/KDE 1.x needs to be removed
> > Date: Tue, 4 Sep 2001 00:15:01 +0300 (EEST)
> >
> >  On Mon, 3 Sep 2001 11:52:13 -0700 (PDT), Will Andrews wrote:
> >  >
> >  > >Description:
> >  >
> >  > KDE 2.x is attaining fruition with the latest release.  Many apps that
> >  > were previously only found in KDE 1.x have since moved to 2.x.  Therefore
> >  > the KDE FreeBSD team should coordinate a full removal of KDE 1.x from
> >  > the FreeBSD ports tree after FreeBSD 4.4-RELEASE.
> >  >
> >  > Same goes for QT 1.x.
> >
> >  While I agree with you regarding KDE 1.x, I think that we can keep
> >  QT 1.4x for some more time, because there are some packages that never
> >  had been updated to work with QT 2.x. After all, maintaining one port
> >  (i.e. QT 1.4x) is not going to be a resource-consuming task, so I
> >  would like to suggest to keep it "just in the case."
> >
> >  -Maxim
>
> Removing of qt1x has one more benefit:  we can that name qt2x libs like
> libqt, not libqt2...

Then rename qt1x libs into libqt1 and leave it alone, or even better
convince Trolls to change naming scheme for major QT releases, so we
wouldn't have to go through it again with each major QT release. GTK
folks already decided to go this way in their gtk-1.3.x (aka gtk 2.0).

-Maxim
